steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize a similarity between kale and carrots. The correct choice must name both vegetables and describe something they share.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Carrots are vegetables that contain ascorbic acid; in fact, there is 6 mg of it in every 100g of carrot.': Discusses only carrots; kale is not mentioned. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Humans cannot make vitamin C in their bodies, so they must get this essential nutrient from vegetables, such as kale.': Mentions only kale as an example; does not address carrots or any shared trait. ✗
  • Choice C — 'One thing that kale and carrots have in common is that they both contain vitamin C.': Explicitly names both vegetables and states their shared property (vitamin C / ascorbic acid). ✓
  • Choice D — 'Just like fruits, vegetables are a food that humans can eat to get ascorbic acid, also known as vitamin C.': Compares vegetables to fruits in general; does not focus on kale and carrots specifically. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
